title: [roserade] Battle Guide
summary: A [grass]/[poison] special attacker and utility lead that amplifies weaker moves with [technician] while spreading [toxic-spikes] and [sleep-powder].
strengths: [grass]/[poison] STAB covers a wide neutral spread
weaknesses: Paper-thin physical bulk collapses to priority
Stacked Ice, [fire], Flying, and Psychic weaknesses
90 Speed falls short of the top unboosted tier

recommendedMoves.reason: Nuclear [grass] STAB that converts predictions into kills
Reliable [poison] STAB for Fairies and mirror [grass]
Match-up theft on the first turn of a lead exchange

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[roserade] is a [grass]/[poison] special attacker built around [technician] boosts and hazard support. 125 SpA with 90 Speed and 105 SpD puts it in the midrange of Champions' special threats — not the fastest, not the bulkiest, but flexible enough to lead, nuke, or pivot depending on the build. The two signature jobs are [leaf-storm] damage dealing and the [toxic-spikes] plus [sleep-powder] lead.

## Strengths
The [grass]/[poison] STAB combo hits Water, [grass], Fairy, and Ground for solid neutral coverage, and [technician] multiplies weaker tools like magical-leaf and hidden-power into meaningful damage. [sleep-powder] is the match-up-theft button that converts awkward 50/50s into free turns for the rest of the team, and [toxic-spikes] quietly taxes every grounded switch the opponent makes. 105 SpD even lets [roserade] trade with special pivots in a pinch.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 60/65/105 defensive line means any priority attack — bullet-punch, ice-shard, sucker-punch — ends [roserade] immediately. Ice, [fire], Flying, and Psychic all hit for 2x, and losing the Speed race to any scarfed attacker is usually a one-shot. Steel switch-ins wall both STABs, so the last move slot has to answer them or [roserade] has to pivot out.

## Recommended Moveset
[leaf-storm] is the nuclear [grass] STAB — one hit usually closes a game when prediction is right. [sludge-bomb] is the click-and-forget [poison] STAB that punishes Fairies and mirror [grass]. [sleep-powder] locks down a target on the lead turn, and [toxic-spikes] taxes the opposing switch plan from the very first entry. Hidden-power ground can replace [toxic-spikes] on an offensive build to answer Steel pivots directly.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the safest lead pick; it guarantees [roserade] survives one shot and gets hazards or sleep down. [choice-scarf] is the offensive alternative, flipping Speed to outrun the mid-90s bracket. [miracle-seed] is the Middle option — it boosts [leaf-storm] without the lock-in cost. [technician] is the must-have ability. Tera [grass] maxes STAB damage, while Tera [fire] flips the Ice and [fire] weaknesses when [roserade] needs to absorb a hit.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair [roserade] with a stealth-rock setter so hazards stack quickly, and bring a Water, Rock, or Steel partner that can absorb the [fire], Ice, and Flying hits aimed at it. A fast setup sweeper loves the [sleep-powder] turn — any partner that can take a free Dragon Dance or Nasty Plot behind sleep snowballs the game. An ally immune to [toxic-spikes] (Steel or [poison]) keeps the hazard game one-sided.

## Play Patterns
On the lead turn, [toxic-spikes] or [sleep-powder] usually takes priority over damage. Only [fire] [leaf-storm] once a switch is forced or a kill is confirmed — the SpA drop leaves [roserade] vulnerable. Save Tera for a telegraphed Ice or [fire] move and scout Steel switch-ins with [sludge-bomb] before committing.
